name: Speedrun starter MCAT pack
note: Hand-authored MCAT-style discrete questions (no AI, no copyrighted source). card_tag links a question to source cards carrying that tag.

questions.stem: Which amino acid is most likely to be buried in the hydrophobic interior of a globular protein in aqueous solution?
questions.explanation: Leucine has a nonpolar aliphatic side chain, so it is favored in the protein interior; the others are charged/polar and prefer the aqueous surface.

questions.stem: An enzyme lowers the activation energy of a reaction. This affects:
options: Both the forward and reverse reaction rates
questions.explanation: Catalysts lower activation energy for both directions equally; they speed attainment of equilibrium without changing K or delta-G.

questions.stem: The bond linking adjacent residues in a protein backbone is best described as:
questions.explanation: The peptide bond is an amide bond between a carboxyl carbon and an amino nitrogen.

questions.stem: A buffer best resists changes in pH when:
options: pH equals the pKa of the weak acid
pH is far above the pKa
pH is far below the pKa
questions.explanation: Buffering capacity is maximal when pH = pKa, where the conjugate acid/base ratio is 1 (Henderson-Hasselbalch).

questions.stem: Raising temperature in an exothermic reaction at equilibrium shifts the system:
options: Toward whichever side has more moles of gas
questions.explanation: For an exothermic reaction heat is a product; by Le Chatelier, added heat shifts equilibrium toward reactants and lowers K.

questions.stem: Which molecule is expected to have the highest normal boiling point?
questions.explanation: Water forms extensive hydrogen bonds, giving it the highest boiling point among these small molecules.

questions.stem: A ball is thrown straight upward. At its highest point, its acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Velocity is momentarily zero at the apex, but gravitational acceleration remains g downward throughout the flight.

questions.stem: If an object's speed doubles, its kinetic energy changes by a factor of:
questions.explanation: Kinetic energy scales with the square of speed (KE = 1/2 m v^2), so doubling v quadruples KE.
